title: FSM Loop Definition
description: Schema for little-loops FSM loop definitions (.loops/*.yaml)

initial.description: Starting state name (must exist in states)

scope.description: Paths this loop operates on (for concurrency control)

input_key.description: Context key used as fallback when the positional input arg is not valid JSON or has no keys matching the loop's defined context variables. When JSON unpacking succeeds, multiple context keys are populated directly and input_key is not used (default: 'input')

required_inputs.description: Context keys that must be non-empty before the loop starts. The runner aborts with exit code 1 if any listed key is absent or empty in fsm.context at start-time.

default_timeout.description: Default timeout for individual states in seconds

config.description: Per-loop ll-config overrides (applied for the duration of this loop's execution)

handoff_threshold.description: Override for LL_HANDOFF_THRESHOLD (context monitor handoff percentage)

readiness_threshold.description: Override for readiness score gate threshold (1-100)

outcome_threshold.description: Override for outcome confidence gate threshold (1-100)

max_continuations.description: Override for maximum number of session continuations
Override for maximum number of session continuations

import.description: Fragment library files to import (paths relative to this loop file's directory)

fragments.description: Named partial state definitions (local fragment library). Keys are fragment names; values are partial state dicts. Local fragments override same-name imported fragments.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this fragment provides and what the calling state must supply. Stripped at parse time — the FSM engine never sees it.

parameters.description: Declares the fragment's public input contract. Callers bind values to these parameters via 'with:' on states that use this fragment.
Declares the loop's public input contract. Callers bind values to these parameters via 'with:' on sub-loop states.

flow.description: Linear-mode shorthand: ordered list of state names defining a chain. Each entry is either a bare name (unconditional transition) or a ternary 'name?yes_target:no_target' (conditional branching). The last entry is implicitly terminal. Mutually exclusive with 'states'.

state_defs.description: State bodies for flow: entries. Keys must match names in the flow: list. Values are partial state dicts (prompt, action, evaluate, etc.) deep-merged into the generated state skeletons.

from.description: Parent loop name for template inheritance. Child inherits all parent fields; child overrides win per deep-merge semantics.

targets.description: Per-FSM-state targeting spec for harness-optimize APO (ENH-1552). Each entry names a loop YAML file (or glob) and the states within it to optimize.

repeated_failure.description: Stall detector for repeated (state, exit_code, verdict) triples. Fires after `window` consecutive iterations produce an identical triple.

window.description: Consecutive identical triples required to fire.

on_repeated_failure.description: Either the literal "abort" (terminate with terminated_by="stall_detected") or the name of a declared state to route to.

progress_paths.description: Paths (supports ${env.PWD} interpolation) to watch for mtime/size changes. When any path changes between consecutive record() calls for the same eval-bearing state, the stall window resets. Opt-in; empty by default preserves existing semantics.

exclude_paths.description: Paths to exclude from the progress_paths fingerprint (BUG-1767). Use this to list the loop's own internal bookkeeping files (plan, DoD, scratchpad) so that appending to them does not reset the stall window. Supports ${env.PWD} interpolation.

stateConfig.description: Configuration for a single FSM state

action.description: Command to execute (shell or slash command)

action_type.description: How to execute the action: prompt (Claude CLI), slash_command (Claude CLI), shell (bash), or mcp_tool (direct MCP tool call), or any contributed extension action type. If omitted, uses heuristic: / prefix = slash_command, else = shell.

params.description: MCP tool arguments (only used with action_type: mcp_tool). Supports ${variable} interpolation in string values.

terminal.description: If true, this is an end state

capture.description: Variable name to store action output

append_to_messages.description: Interpolation expression whose result is appended to the shared run-scoped messages log (e.g. '${captured.execute.output}')

on_maintain.description: State to transition to when maintain=true and loop completes

max_retries.description: Max consecutive re-entries before transitioning to on_retry_exhausted. A value of N allows N retries after the initial execution (N+1 total entries). Requires on_retry_exhausted.

on_retry_exhausted.description: State to transition to when max_retries consecutive re-entries are exceeded. Required when max_retries is set.

retryable_exit_codes.description: Optional filter: only these exit codes trigger a retry when on_error routes back to the same state. Non-matching codes bypass the retry counter and route to on_retry_exhausted (if set) or on_error directly. Requires on_error.

max_rate_limit_retries.description: Max consecutive 429/rate-limit in-place retries for this state before transitioning to on_rate_limit_exhausted. Requires on_rate_limit_exhausted.

on_rate_limit_exhausted.description: State to transition to when max_rate_limit_retries consecutive rate-limit retries are exhausted. Required when max_rate_limit_retries is set.

rate_limit_backoff_base_seconds.description: Base seconds for exponential backoff between rate-limit retries; actual sleep = base * 2^(attempt-1) + uniform(0, base).

rate_limit_max_wait_seconds.description: Total wall-clock budget (seconds) for rate-limit handling in this state before routing to on_rate_limit_exhausted. Defaults from commands.rate_limits.max_wait_seconds (21600 = 6h).

rate_limit_long_wait_ladder.description: Backoff ladder (seconds) for the long-wait tier used after the short-tier retry budget is spent. Defaults from commands.rate_limits.long_wait_ladder ([300, 900, 1800, 3600]).

loop.description: Name of a loop YAML to execute as a sub-FSM. Mutually exclusive with action.

context_passthrough.description: Pass parent context variables to child loop and merge child captures back into parent context. Legacy escape hatch; prefer 'with' for explicit named bindings.

with.description: Explicit parameter bindings for sub-loop calls or fragment parameter binding. Maps declared parameter names to expressions (supports ${variable} interpolation). Valid when 'loop' is set (sub-loop binding) or 'fragment' is set (fragment parameter binding). Mutually exclusive with 'context_passthrough'.

fragment.description: Name of a fragment to inherit fields from. Fragment fields are deep-merged into the state; state-level keys override fragment keys at every nesting level. Consumed at parse time — the engine never sees this key.

agent.description: Agent name to pass as --agent to the Claude CLI subprocess. Only used for prompt-mode states (action_type: prompt or slash_command). Ignored for shell-mode states.

tools.description: List of tool names to pass as --tools CSV to the Claude CLI subprocess. Only used for prompt-mode states. Ignored for shell-mode states.

model.description: Model override to pass as --model to the Claude CLI subprocess for this state only. Only used for prompt-mode states (action_type: prompt or slash_command). Ignored for shell/mcp_tool/contract states — a validation WARNING is emitted if set on those types. Absent = inherits the global --model flag or host default.

type.description: Optional state type marker. Set to 'learning' (FEAT-1283) to dispatch to the learning-state handler: queries the learning-tests registry for each target, invokes /ll:explore-api on miss/stale, routes to on_yes when all proven and on_blocked/on_no on refuted/retries-exhausted. Learning states are also exempt from throttle hard_max enforcement.

learning.description: FEAT-1283: configuration for type=learning states. Declares targets that must be proven against the learning-tests registry (ENH-1282) before the state advances. Required when type='learning'. Exactly one of 'targets' or 'targets_csv' must be set.

targets.description: Ordered list of target identifiers (e.g. 'Anthropic SDK streaming'). Each is slugified internally when looking up registry records. All targets must reach status='proven' for the state to advance via on_yes.

targets_csv.description: Comma-separated target identifiers resolved at runtime via interpolate(). Alternative to 'targets' for loops that pass targets as a context CSV string (e.g., "${context.targets}").

max_retries.description: Maximum /ll:explore-api invocations per target before routing to on_blocked/on_no with reason 'retries_exhausted'. Counts only re-exploration attempts; the initial registry lookup is free.

max_retries_expr.description: Runtime-interpolated retry limit. Resolved via interpolate() and int()-cast at execution time. Alternative to the static 'max_retries' integer for loops that pass max_retries as a context string (e.g., "${context.max_retries}"). Takes precedence over max_retries when set.

throttle.description: Per-state progressive tool-call throttling configuration. Counts successive action executions within a single state visit and escalates restrictions. All fields optional; defaults from executor module constants (normal_max=3, warn_max=8, hard_max=12).

normal_max.description: Calls 1..normal_max pass through without restriction. Default: 3.

warn_max.description: At this call count, a throttle_warn event is emitted. Default: 8.

hard_max.description: At this call count, transition to on_throttle_hard (or on_error). Calls beyond hard_max hard-stop the loop. Default: 12.

on_throttle_hard.description: State to transition to when throttle.hard_max consecutive tool calls are made in a single state visit. If not set, falls back to on_error.

^on_.description: Custom on_<verdict> shorthand routing for llm_structured evaluators with non-standard verdict strings

evaluateConfig.description: Evaluator configuration for action result interpretation

negate.description: If true, invert the match result (output_contains)

schema.description: Custom JSON schema for llm_structured response

uncertain_suffix.description: If true, append _uncertain to low-confidence verdicts

scope.description: Paths to limit git diff to (for diff_stall evaluator)

max_stall.description: Consecutive no-change iterations before stall verdict (diff_stall)

track.description: Context keys to track for action_stall (default: ["action"])

max_repeat.description: Consecutive identical iterations before stall verdict (action_stall)

auto_promote.description: If true, write harness output to baseline when verdict is yes

min_pairs.description: Number of blind A/B comparison pairs to run (majority vote)

pairs.description: Producer/consumer file pairs for contract evaluator

contract.description: Alignment rule for the LLM judge to enforce

producer_pattern.description: Optional regex to extract relevant slice from producer

consumer_pattern.description: Optional regex to extract relevant slice from consumer

parameterSpec.description: Specification for a single loop input parameter declared in the 'parameters:' block

required.description: If true, callers must supply this parameter in 'with:'. Mutually exclusive with 'default'.

default.description: Default value when the parameter is not bound by the caller. Only valid when 'required' is false.

enabled.description: If false, disable LLM evaluation entirely

model.description: Model identifier for LLM calls (default: DEFAULT_LLM_MODEL from schema.py)

timeout.description: Timeout for LLM calls in seconds

targetStateSpec.description: Per-state optimization spec: names one FSM state and its associated examples file and eval fragment.

name.description: State name within the target loop

examples_file.description: Path to the examples YAML file for this state

eval.description: Eval fragment identifier used during optimization

targetFileSpec.description: Associates a loop YAML file (or glob pattern) with the states to optimize.

file.description: Explicit path to a loop YAML file (mutually exclusive with glob)

glob.description: Glob pattern matching one or more loop YAML files

states.description: States within the matched file(s) to target
